replies.author: @NovaTerrain-4K
replies.text: The segment on Caernarfon Castle [05:45] was profound. King Edward I built this massive stone fortress to crush independence and enforce assimilation, yet the Welsh responded right at its gates with the Mari Lwyd winter traditionвЂ”using a horse skull and satirical poetry to challenge authority. Did this ultimate contrast between colonial stone power and stubborn folk wisdom make you admire the resilient spirit of the Welsh?

comments.author: @TerraVista-4K
comments.text: What a beautiful, deeply moving tribute to Wales! ItвЂ™s rare to find a documentary that captures not just the stunning landscapes, but the true soul, resilience, and language of a nation. From the moving history of Aberfan to the haunting beauty of the cliffs, this was absolute perfection. Thank you for showing what makes Britain's quietest nation so incredibly special!

replies.author: @PlanetVista4K
replies.text: Thank you for such a thoughtful comment; we are so glad the soul and history of Wales resonated with you! рџЏґу Ѓ§у Ѓўу Ѓ·у Ѓ¬у Ѓіу Ѓї
